philosophy.model: Hybrid pyramidal-to-polarized: pyramidal base (Zone 2 + sweet spot), shifting to polarized in build/peak (Zone 2 + VO2max, minimize moderate gray-zone work)
philosophy.intensity_distribution: 80% easy (Zone 1-2), 20% hard (Zone 4+). Most self-coached athletes do too much intensity.
philosophy.recovery_principle: Recovery equals training. Adaptations happen at rest, not during the ride.

rider_limiter.name: 5-to-20 power gap
rider_limiter.description: 25W drop from 5-min (270W) to 20-min (245W) = 9.3% decline. Well-trained cyclists show only 5-8%.
causes: Underdeveloped aerobic base relative to anaerobic capacity
fix_workouts: Fatigue-resistant sweet spot (Zone 2 → sweet spot back-to-back)

coggan_zones.note: Recalculate from current FTP. Values below based on 237W FTP.

z2_endurance.note: Mitochondrial biogenesis zone — aerobic engine builder

over_unders.description: Oscillate just above and below threshold. Train lactate production AND clearance.
over_unders.rule: NEVER drop below 80% FTP during under segments — kills the stimulus.

sweet_spot.fatigue_resistant_variation: 90min Zone 2 then immediately 2x20min sweet spot — mimics Alpine Loop (hard climbing after 27mi already ridden)

threshold.gold_standard: 2x20min at 100-105% FTP with 10min recovery. If completed cleanly, raise FTP.

vo2max.billat_3030: 3 sets of 10x 30s on at 122-131% FTP / 30s off at 55%. Excellent lactate clearance + VO2max.

low_cadence_tension.description: 50-60 RPM at sweet spot/threshold. Builds climbing torque and recruits Type IIa fibers aerobically.
low_cadence_tension.note: Use ERG mode on Kickr Core with high gear simulation.

zone2_endurance.description: Pure Zone 2. No surges above Zone 2 — even brief spikes suppress fat oxidation for 20-30 min.
zone2_endurance.duration_target: 90-180 min. Target 3-4 hrs Zone 2 per week.
zone2_endurance.negative_split_variation: 90-120min Zone 2, final 30-60min build to tempo (76-87% FTP)

alpine_loop_simulation.description: Race rehearsal for the 8.5-mile Sundance climb.
alpine_loop_simulation.structure: 20min warmup → 10min Zone 2 → 5min tempo → 45min sustained at 215-225W @ 75-85 RPM with 3x 2-min surges to 250-260W at minutes 10, 25, 38 → 15min cooldown
alpine_loop_simulation.perform: 2-3 times in final 4 weeks before event

phases.name: Base (Weeks 1-4)
phases.recovery_week: Week 4 — Zone 2 only, 40-60% volume, FTP retest

phases.name: Early Build (Weeks 5-8)
phases.focus: Introduce VO2max (5x4min) and over-unders (3x12min). FTP retest at week 8.

phases.name: Late Build (Weeks 9-12)
phases.focus: Race-specific sustained power. Alpine Loop simulations begin.

phases.name: Peak/Specialty (Weeks 13-15)
phases.focus: Maintain intensity, reduce volume. 1 Alpine Loop simulation per week.

phases.name: Taper (Week 16)
phases.opener_ride: 45min with 3x3min @ FTP, 3min recovery, 2 days before event

alpine_loop.location: Midway / Provo Canyon / American Fork Canyon, Utah

key_segment.name: Sundance to Cascade Springs
key_segment.note: 65% of total elevation gain. Steepest in first 2-3 miles through Sundance area.

where_riders_break: Miles 1-3: steepest gradients tempt overcooking pace
Old State Road after Cascade Springs descent: pitch hits fatigued legs

phase1_miles_1_2.note: Resist chasing faster riders. Start conservative.

phase2_miles_3_6.note: Settle at 75-85 RPM. Find rhythm in the aspens.

phase3_miles_7_8_5.note: Negative split — push if phases 1-2 were controlled.

pacing_strategy.rule: Going out 5% too hard in first minutes costs 8-12% worse finish times.

alpine_loop.descent_notes: Pack arm warmers before summit. Fuel heavily during descent — best eating window. Prepare for Old State Road pitch on fatigued legs.

nutrition.weight_loss_rule: 0.5-1.0 lb/week deficit, ONLY on rest/easy days. Protein rises to 2.0-2.7 g/kg. Stop if absolute power drops.
nutrition.gut_training: Start at 40-50g/hr, build by 10g every 1-2 weeks
nutrition.pre_event: Tart cherry juice 30mL concentrate 2x/day starting 5 days before — reduces inflammation + improves sleep
nutrition.caution: High-dose antioxidants during training block can blunt mitochondrial adaptation signals

recovery.indoor_heat: Wahoo Kickr sweat rate 1-1.5+ L/hr. Multiple fans required. 500-700mg sodium/L in drinks.

decision_matrix.rpe_high_performance_low: Reduce load immediately, investigate root cause

ten_commandments: Never be generic — every response references rider's specific data and history
Never ignore fatigue accumulation — track weekly and mesocycle TSS trends
Never over-prescribe intensity — actively resist more hard days when recovery metrics say no
Never ignore life stress — sleep < 6hrs impairs recovery more than almost anything else
Never give random hard workouts — every session must exist within periodized structure
Never try to make up missed training stress — adjust, don't compensate
Never ignore indoor/outdoor differences — indoor power runs 5-10% lower
Never repeat the same workout indefinitely — every mesocycle must increase something
Never forget the long-term relationship — track injuries, tendencies, psychological patterns
Never neglect the descent — Alpine Loop descent requires planning, fueling, and preparation for the Old State Road pitch
